rows.notes: Title at top of page. Handwriting legible though Latin abbreviations present; no monetary amounts present.

rows.description: Rectoris a 2do die Novembris 1814 ad eundem
rows.notes: Second line of heading; some letters (e.g. '2do') are abbreviated but overall readable; no amounts.
Heading indicating the accounting year. Ink is clear; no monetary values on this line.

Description legible as 'Arrearia recepta'. Right-hand columns show '613' in pounds column, '10' in shillings column, and '1' in pence column; vertical alignment with ruling lines clear; values within valid ranges.
Description reads as 'Bankers' (handwriting clear). Amounts aligned clearly to right: 38 pounds, 7 shillings, 4 pence; all values within valid ranges.

Description interpreted as 'Receipts Varia' (handwriting slightly slanted). Amount columns show '1194' (pounds), '2' (shillings), and '11' with a small symbol following (interpreted as a farthing, 0.25). Column boundaries checked: shillings and pence are valid.

Subtotal line with only amounts (no description). Amount reads as 1816 pounds, 16 shillings, 4 and 3/4 pence (the pence has a clear '3q'/'3/4' style mark). Column alignment used for placement; pence fraction interpreted as 0.75 though the handwritten fraction is somewhat faint.

Description readable as Latin 'Debita anni praecedentis'. Amount appears as 15 pounds, 4 shillings, 2 with a small 'ob' style loop suggesting a halfpenny (0.5). Fraction interpretation moderate confidence due to small loop.

Line reads 'Summa Receptorum' (heading for total receipts). Amount printed as '2001 - 7 -' on the page; interpretation: 2001 pounds, 0 shillings, 7 pence. This interpretation is supported by later balancing arithmetic on the page (see subtraction that yields 303.1.6). Vertical alignment of the '2001' and the '7' to the pounds/pence columns is clear.
New section header for payments ('Soluta pro hoc anno'). Handwriting of header clear; no monetary amounts on this line.

Description reads as payments to 'Rectori, Scholari, & Officiar' (letters legible). Amount columns clearly show 661 pounds, 5 shillings, 7 pence; values validated against column rulings and within valid ranges.

Abbreviated description 'Schol: Ordinari' (clear abbreviation). Amount reads 153 pounds, 18 shillings, 2 pence; alignment with shillings column confirmed and shillings value (18) is within valid 0-19 range.
Description 'Varia' (short and clear). Amount clearly reads 882 pounds, 15 shillings, 4 pence. Alignment and digit clarity good.

Subtotal / Sum line for payments (no description text printed). Amount reads 1697 pounds, 19 shillings, 1 pence; this total is arithmetically consistent with the three payment lines above (661.5.7 + 153.18.2 + 882.15.4 = 1697.19.1). Column alignment clear.

This is a repeated/recapitulation line reading 'Receptis' followed by the same amount previously seen (2001 - 7 -). Position lower on page but numerals are the same; alignment with pounds and pence columns is clear, interpreted again as 2001 pounds, 0 shillings, 7 pence. Matches earlier receipt total and used in final balancing.
Deduction line (labelled 'Deduct. Sol:') with amount 1697.19.1. This matches the payments subtotal above; digits and column placement are clear.

Balance carried forward / remainder. Amount reads 303 pounds, 1 shilling, 6 pence. This value is arithmetically consistent with 2001.0.7 minus 1697.19.1 (borrowing and column arithmetic checked). Numerals are clear and well aligned.
Section heading introducing the attesting signatures ('Computus completatus per nos:'). Handwriting clear; no amounts.
Signature/name line. Interpreted as 'J. Cole Rectorum' — letters are cursive and somewhat compressed; no monetary figures present.
Second signature line; read as 'J. C. Jones' followed by an abbreviation (interpreted as 'Subnitorum' or 'Substitorem'). Handwriting is less clear; no amounts.
Signature appears to read 'Tho Smyth Glubb' with title 'Burs. Sen.' (Bursar Senior). Letters fairly legible though some flourishes; no monetary values.
Signature read as 'J. L. Heyes' with title 'Burs. Jun.' (Bursar Junior). Handwriting legible but small; no amounts.
Name line probably 'J. Roodes' or similar followed by 'Decanum' (dean). Handwritten letters are cramped and some characters ambiguous; no monetary amounts.
Final attesting name, read as 'Gul. Ed. Hony M.A.' (abbreviated Latinized name with degree). Cursive is legible enough to extract initials and degree but full name uncertain; no amounts.
